subject: Sociology
title: Globalization and Transnational Crime
description: A short lesson to help you apply Globalization and Transnational Crime.

hook.text: Drug cartels operate across continents. Human traffickers use global networks. Crime went multinational.

buildup.text: Transnational crime exploits open borders, global finance, and digital communication that states can't control.

discovery.text: Criminal networks mirror legal business: supply chains, logistics, money laundering through global banking.

twist.text: Fighting transnational crime requires cooperation between nations that often distrust each other's motives.

climax.text: Globalization didn't just connect markets. It connected criminal enterprises beyond any single state's reach.

punchline.text: Crime globalized faster than justice did.

quiz.question: Why is transnational crime hard to combat?
options: It crosses borders and exploits gaps between national systems
